title: cephaelis peduncularis var. ivorensis
intro: Cephaelis peduncularis var. ivorensis, also known as Ivorian quinine tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is native to West Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-malarial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been isolated and studied for their potential in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cephaelis peduncularis var. ivorensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and prevent malaria, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
